Off Calendar Nov-15-2021 Continued To Dec-20-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-06-2021 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Consolidate Actions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Seth M. Skootsky; [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Jun-28-2021 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Order
Off Calendar Jun-28-2021 Continued To Oct-12-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Seth Skootsky appeared by zoom for and with Benjamin Bass by zoom, and for Defendants in the civil action.
Tom Drohan and Darren Orr appeared by zoom for Heidi Bass (Settlor).
Liza de Vries appeared by zoom for and with Daniel Cunningham (Trustee) by zoom.
Arthur Khurin appeared by zoom for Defendants.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions regarding whether or not it is appropriate to consolidate the probate matter with the civil matter. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court granted the Motion to Consolidate Actions. Case number CGC-20-582398 is designated the lead case. The Petition for Order Confirming that Real Property had been an Asset of the Trust Since July 18, 2014, etc. is to be tried with the civil matter. Status of any remaining issues that are not decided at trial is set for November 15, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.

Petition For Order
Off Calendar May-26-2021 Continued To Jun-28-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Seth Skootsky, via ZOOM, counsel for Benjamin Bass, Petitioner; Darren Orr and Thomas Drohan, via ZOOM, counsel for Heidi Bass, Settlor; Liza DeVries, via ZOOM, counsel for Daniel Cunningham, Successor Trustee.

At the outset, the court discusses with counsel regarding settlement discussions. Mr. Orr confirms that the parties had one mediation session with Betty Epstein in 2019 and one informal meeting amongst counsel in 2020. The court then turns to the pending, related civil matter which is set in Department 206 for trial assignment within the next few months. Mr. Skootsky advises that the trial in the civil matter has been continued to October 12, 2021 and the parties have discussed possibly filing a motion to consolidate the civil case with the trust matter and asks the court for guidance as to which Department the motion should be scheduled in. The court advises Mr. Skootsky that the parties can file the appropriate papers with regards to consolidating the matters with the probate court and it will be considered at the next hearing, if filed.

After additional discussion, the Amended Petition for Order, etc., is continued to June 28, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

***After discussion in court following the hearing, the clerk will advise counsel via email to include the wording "approved by Robert" next to the June 28,2021 hearing date and time on the papers regarding consolidation, if papers are filed.

Petition For Order
Off Calendar Apr-28-2021 Continued To May-26-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order
Off Calendar Mar-17-2021 Continued To Apr-28-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
